opinion.h4: SCOTT v. PENNSYLVANIA. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF PENNSYLVANIA. No. 1003, Misc. Decided June 10, 1963.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Walter E. Alessandroni, Attorney General of Pennsylvania, and George G. Lindsay, Assistant Attorney General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
opinion.h4: SLOAN'S MOVING & STORAGE CO., INC., v. UNITED STATES, 374 U.S. 95 (1963)
opinion.p: 374 U.S. 95 SLOAN'S MOVING & STORAGE CO., INC., v. UNITED STATES ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE EASTERN DISTRICT OF MISSOURI. No. 880. Decided June 10, 1963.

Solicitor General Cox, Assistant Attorney General Loevinger, Robert B. Hummel, Robert W. Ginnane and H. Neil Garson for the United States and the Interstate Commerce Commission; and Bernard G. Segal, Richmond C. Coburn, S. Harrison Kahn, Irving R. Segal and Robert L. Kendall, Jr. for United Parcel Service, Inc., appellees.
The motions to affirm are granted and the judgment is affirmed.
